title: Rest is not laziness
subtitle: A short affirmation for uncoupling rest from guilt.
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 13 of the SLEEP program. Used to interrupt the automatic guilt that fires when you stop, the internal voice that confuses resting with failing.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 13 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

content.text: A short affirmation from Day 13 of the SLEEP program. One sentence to use as a direct counter to the guilt voice, not a debate, just a quieter statement put alongside the louder one.
Rest is not laziness. Rest is how I sustain everything I care about.

Say it out loud three times in the morning. Say it again in any moment when the guilt arrives: you closed the laptop, you lay down, you chose sleep over the list. Two sentences, said once, hand on chest. You are not arguing with the guilt. You are offering a different instruction.
Adapted from Day 13 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
